title: FastAPI app skeleton + serve CLI command
analysis:
This task creates the web management console skeleton for autopilot-video. The project already has a Click CLI (autopilot/cli.py) with group + subcommands pattern, a CatalogDB class in autopilot/db.py with WAL mode and full pipeline control tables (from task 35), and a mature test suite using pytest with CliRunner and conftest fixtures.

Key observations:
1. The CLI uses click.Group with @main.command() decorators, _common_options decorator, and _setup_context helper for config+DB loading. The 'serve' command does NOT need _common_options (no --dry-run, --force, etc.) — it needs its own --host, --port, --output-dir options.
2. CatalogDB takes a db_path string and sets up WAL mode. The web app factory needs to accept a db_path and store it for lazy connection creation.
3. pyproject.toml uses hatchling build, has [project.scripts] autopilot = 'autopilot.cli:main'. Dependencies need fastapi, uvicorn[standard], jinja2, python-multipart added.
4. Task description says create_app(db_path) — the app factory pattern. The serve CLI command constructs the path from output_dir.
5. The existing test_scaffolding.py checks for __init__.py in all packages — adding autopilot/web will need __init__.py.
6. Tests should use FastAPI's TestClient (from httpx) for testing without starting uvicorn.
7. The serve command in the task description has output_dir / 'catalog.db' which needs Path conversion since click.Path() returns a string.
8. Lazy imports in serve command follow the pattern already used in other subcommands (e.g., importing PipelineOrchestrator at module top is fine, but uvicorn should be lazy since it's only needed for serve).

Review fix: The SSE notification listener in app.js line 36 does JSON.parse(event.data) without try/catch. Malformed or truncated SSE payloads would throw an uncaught SyntaxError, silently dropping the notification. Need to wrap in try/catch with console.error logging.

prerequisites.description: Create autopilot/web/ package structure: autopilot/web/__init__.py, autopilot/web/routes/__init__.py. Create empty directories for templates/ and static/.

Add web dependencies to pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], jinja2, python-multipart. Add httpx to [project.optional-dependencies] dev list for TestClient. Run uv lock to update uv.lock.

steps.description: Write failing test in tests/test_web_app.py: TestCreateApp.test_create_app_returns_fastapi_instance — import create_app from autopilot.web.app, call it with a tmp_path db_path, assert returned object is a FastAPI instance.

Implement autopilot/web/app.py with create_app(db_path) factory that returns a FastAPI instance. Configure Jinja2Templates pointing at templates dir. Mount static files at /static. Store db_path in app.state.db_path for later use.
Write failing test: TestHealthCheck.test_health_returns_200_ok — use httpx TestClient on create_app(tmp_path db), GET /api/health, assert status 200 and JSON body {'status': 'ok'}.
Add GET /api/health route to the app in app.py that returns {'status': 'ok'}.
Write failing test: TestStaticFiles.test_app_css_served — use TestClient, GET /static/app.css, assert 200 response. TestStaticFiles.test_app_js_served — GET /static/app.js, assert 200.

Create autopilot/web/static/app.css with minimal custom styles (dark theme overrides if needed) and autopilot/web/static/app.js with SSE connection handler stub, notification support, and toast display helper.

Write failing test: TestBaseTemplate.test_base_template_contains_tailwind_cdn — add a test route to the app that renders base.html with Jinja2, GET it via TestClient, assert response HTML contains tailwindcss CDN script tag. TestBaseTemplate.test_base_template_contains_htmx — assert htmx CDN script present. TestBaseTemplate.test_base_template_has_nav_items — assert nav contains Dashboard, Media, Pipeline, Review, Gates, Settings. TestBaseTemplate.test_base_template_has_notification_bell — assert bell placeholder present. TestBaseTemplate.test_base_template_has_toast_container — assert toast container div present. TestBaseTemplate.test_base_template_has_dark_theme — assert dark theme class/attribute present.

Create autopilot/web/templates/base.html — Jinja2 base template with: Tailwind CSS CDN play script, HTMX CDN (<14KB), full nav (Dashboard, Media, Pipeline with Stage Detail + Jobs sub-items, Review with Clusters + Narratives + Scripts + Edit Plans + Renders sub-items, Gates, Settings), notification bell placeholder in nav, {% block content %} main area, toast container div, dark theme using Tailwind dark classes and appropriate media workstation styling.

Write failing test: TestServeCommand.test_serve_registered_in_main — assert 'serve' in main.commands. test_serve_has_host_option — assert --host with default '127.0.0.1'. test_serve_has_port_option — assert --port with default 8080, type int. test_serve_has_output_dir_option — assert --output-dir option exists.

Add 'autopilot serve' command to autopilot/cli.py: @main.command() with --host (default='127.0.0.1'), --port (default=8080, type=int), --output-dir (type=click.Path(), required=True). Lazy-import uvicorn and create_app. Construct db_path as Path(output_dir) / 'catalog.db'. Call uvicorn.run(app, host=host, port=port).

Write failing test: TestServeCommand.test_serve_invokes_uvicorn — use CliRunner, mock uvicorn.run and autopilot.web.app.create_app, invoke 'serve --output-dir <tmp_path>', assert uvicorn.run called with the app from create_app, host='127.0.0.1', port=8080. test_serve_passes_custom_host_port — invoke with --host 0.0.0.0 --port 9090, verify passed through to uvicorn.run.
Fix any issues from test_serve tests. Ensure serve command correctly passes db_path to create_app and host/port to uvicorn.run. Run full test suite to verify no regressions.

Write failing test: TestSSEErrorHandling.test_sse_notification_listener_has_try_catch — Read the content of autopilot/web/static/app.js, locate the SSE notification event listener (the addEventListener('notification', ...) block), and assert that the JSON.parse call is wrapped in a try/catch. Specifically: assert 'try' and 'catch' appear within the notification listener function body, and that 'console.error' is present in the catch block for logging parse failures. This is a static analysis test of the JS source since browser-level SSE testing is out of scope.

Fix autopilot/web/static/app.js: Wrap the SSE notification listener body in try/catch. Replace the bare `const data = JSON.parse(event.data); showToast(data.message, data.type || 'info');` with `try { const data = JSON.parse(event.data); showToast(data.message, data.type || 'info'); } catch (e) { console.error('SSE notification parse error:', e, event.data); }`. This prevents uncaught SyntaxError from malformed/truncated SSE payloads and logs the error for debugging.

design_decisions.decision: Use FastAPI TestClient (backed by httpx) for testing rather than starting a real server
design_decisions.rationale: TestClient provides synchronous HTTP testing without binding to a port, making tests fast and reliable. httpx is required by starlette's TestClient.

design_decisions.decision: Store db_path in app.state rather than opening a CatalogDB connection at app creation time
design_decisions.rationale: Allows the app factory to work without an actual database file (useful for testing static/template tests). Actual DB connection can be created lazily via FastAPI dependencies in subsequent tasks.

design_decisions.decision: The serve command does NOT use _common_options decorator
design_decisions.rationale: The serve command doesn't need --input-dir, --verbose, --dry-run, --force flags. It only needs --host, --port, and --output-dir. It also doesn't load config or create an orchestrator — it's a fundamentally different command type.

design_decisions.decision: Health check route defined inline in app.py rather than in routes/
design_decisions.rationale: The health check is a fundamental app concern, not a domain route. Keeping it in app.py avoids over-engineering. Domain routes (dashboard, media, pipeline) will go in routes/ per subsequent tasks.

design_decisions.decision: Add httpx to dev dependencies for TestClient support
design_decisions.rationale: FastAPI's TestClient requires httpx. Adding it to dev deps ensures test infrastructure works.

design_decisions.decision: Use Tailwind CDN play script (no build step) and HTMX CDN
design_decisions.rationale: Per task spec — no Node.js toolchain needed. Tailwind play CDN and HTMX CDN (<14KB) are sufficient for a management console.

design_decisions.decision: Convert output_dir to Path before joining with 'catalog.db' in serve command
design_decisions.rationale: click.Path() returns a string by default. Path(output_dir) / 'catalog.db' is needed for path construction.

design_decisions.decision: Use lazy imports for uvicorn and create_app inside serve command body
design_decisions.rationale: Follows existing CLI pattern of importing heavy dependencies inside command functions. Avoids loading FastAPI/uvicorn when running other CLI commands like ingest, analyze, etc.

design_decisions.decision: Test template rendering by adding a temporary test route rather than testing Jinja2 directly
design_decisions.rationale: Testing through the HTTP stack via TestClient ensures the full rendering pipeline works (Jinja2 configuration, template discovery, static file references). More realistic than inspecting template strings.

design_decisions.decision: Test SSE error handling via static JS source analysis rather than browser-level testing
design_decisions.rationale: Browser-level SSE testing would require a headless browser (Playwright/Selenium), which is out of scope for this task. Reading the JS source and asserting the try/catch pattern exists in the notification listener is a pragmatic and reliable verification approach.

reuse.what: CatalogDB class with WAL mode and pipeline control tables
reuse.how: The web app stores db_path in app.state for CatalogDB(db_path) access. Pipeline tables (gates, jobs, events, runs from task 35) will be queried by subsequent dashboard routes.
Add 'serve' as another @main.command() following the existing pattern of subcommand registration.
project_root fixture resolves template/static paths. catalog_db provides in-memory DB for testing.

reuse.what: CliRunner test pattern with mock patches
reuse.how: Follow same CliRunner + mock pattern for testing the serve command — mock uvicorn.run to avoid actually starting a server.

reuse.what: pyproject.toml dependency structure (stable vs ML deps)
reuse.how: Add web deps (fastapi, jinja2, uvicorn, python-multipart) as stable deps without upper bounds, following existing convention for stable ecosystem packages.
